description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Save three beginner safety articles under 8 minutes to Favorites
tasks.description: Find boating safety articles for beginners that are 8 minutes or shorter to read and rated 4.0+ stars, then add the first three matching articles to your Favorites list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Safety Basics" link in the top navigation bar.
In the sidebar filters, click the tag filter labeled "Beginner" to show only beginner-level articles.
In the "Read Time" filter, set the maximum read time to 8 minutes (e.g., by moving the slider to 8 or typing "8" into the max field).
In the "Rating" filter, select the option that shows only articles with "4.0+ stars".
Click the sort dropdown above the article list and select "Highest Rated".
In the resulting article list, click the "Add to Favorites" icon on the first article card that appears.

Still in the same filtered and sorted list, click the "Add to Favorites" icon on the second and third article cards so that you have added a total of three beginner safety articles under 8 minutes to your Favorites.

tasks.name: Complete and save a pontoon day trip safety checklist for 4 people
tasks.description: Locate a pontoon boat safety checklist for a day trip with 4–6 passengers, mark the first 5 checklist items as completed, and save the checklist.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Checklists" option in the top navigation.
On the Checklists page, find the "Boat Type" filter and select "Pontoon" from the dropdown or list.
In the "Trip Length" filter, choose the option labeled "Day Trip (4–8 hours)".
In the "Passengers" dropdown, select the range "4–6 people".
In the filtered checklist results, click the checklist titled "Pontoon Day Trip Safety Checklist".
On the checklist page, scroll to the first section (for example, "Before You Leave Dock") until you can see at least the first five checklist items.
Click the checkbox next to each of the first five checklist items to mark all five as completed.
After marking the items, click the "Save Checklist" button at the top or bottom of the page to save your progress for this pontoon day trip checklist.

tasks.name: Use the boat capacity calculator and save a trip plan for 6 passengers
tasks.description: Use the boat capacity calculator tool with specific boat dimensions and settings, then save the calculated capacity into a named trip plan for six passengers.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Tools" link in the top navigation bar.
On the Tools page, click the card or link labeled "Boat Capacity Calculator".
On the calculator form, click into the length field and type "18" as the boat length in feet.
Click into the width/beam field and type "7" as the boat width in feet.
In the "Boat Type" dropdown, select "Open motorboat".
In the "Water Type" dropdown, select "Inland lake/river".
Click the "Calculate Capacity" button to have the tool compute the recommended maximum capacity.
Next to the displayed maximum passenger capacity result, click the button labeled "Add to Trip Plan".
When a trip plan form appears, type "Weekend Lake Trip for 6" into the trip name field and then click the "Save Trip Plan" button.

tasks.name: Find the newest thunderstorm emergency guide for boats under 26 ft and open print view
tasks.description: Search for thunderstorm emergency guidance articles for boats up to 26 ft, filter by date from 2022-01-01 onward, and open the print-friendly version of the newest matching article.
steps: Click on the search bar in the website header.
Type "thunderstorm emergency" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
On the search results page, open the "Category" filter and select "Safety Basics".
In the "Boat Size" filter dropdown, choose the option labeled "Up to 26 ft".
In the date range filter, set the "From" field to "2022-01-01" and leave the "To" field as it is.
Click the sort dropdown above the results list and select "Newest First".
Click the title of the first article in the sorted results list (the most recent thunderstorm emergency guide for boats under 26 ft).
On the article page, locate the button labeled "Print-Friendly View" near the title or share options and click it to open the simplified print view of the article.

tasks.name: Add the cheapest basic boating safety course under $100 within 25 miles to your training plan
tasks.description: Use the course directory to find basic boating safety courses within 25 miles, filter to prices under $100, then add the cheapest beginner-level course to your training plan with a June 2024 start month.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Courses" option in the top navigation.
On the Courses page, use the course type filter to select "Basic Boating Safety".
In the distance filter field, type "25" and ensure the unit is set to "miles".
In the price filter, set the maximum price to "100" (for $100).
Click the sort dropdown above the course list and choose "Price: Low to High".
In the sorted results, click the title of the first course that shows a price of $100 or less and indicates a level of "Beginner".
On the course detail page, click the "Add to My Training Plan" button.
In the small form or popup, open the start month dropdown and select "June 2024" as the planned start month.
Click the "Save to Plan" button to add this cheapest qualifying course to your training plan.

tasks.name: Set up a monthly maintenance schedule for a 2-stroke engine in saltwater
tasks.description: Use the engine maintenance scheduler tool for a 2-stroke outboard used monthly in saltwater, starting on 2024-07-01, and save the generated monthly maintenance schedule.
steps: Click the "Tools" link in the top navigation from the homepage.
On the Tools page, click the card titled "Engine Maintenance Scheduler".
In the scheduler form, open the "Engine Type" dropdown and select "2-stroke outboard".
In the "Usage Frequency" dropdown, select "Used monthly".
In the "Water Type" dropdown, choose "Saltwater".
Click into the "Start Date" field and enter "2024-07-01".
In the "Reminder Frequency" dropdown, choose "Every 1 month".
Click the "Generate Schedule" button to create the maintenance plan.
On the page showing the generated maintenance schedule, click the "Save Schedule" button so it is stored in your saved tools or schedules area.

tasks.name: Build a three-step night boating learning path from different categories
tasks.description: Create a custom learning path by adding one night navigation article, one beginner anchoring article, and one VHF radio article from three different categories, then open the combined learning path view.
steps: From the homepage, click the "How-To Guides" link in the top navigation.
On the How-To Guides page, click the category tab labeled "Navigation".
Scroll the Navigation article list and click the "Add to Learning Path" icon on the first article whose title contains the word "night" or the phrase "after dark".
Use the breadcrumb or top navigation to return to the main "How-To Guides" listing page.
On the category tabs, click the one labeled "Anchoring & Mooring".
In the Anchoring & Mooring list, click the "Add to Learning Path" icon on the first article card that has a "Beginner" tag displayed.
Return again to the main "How-To Guides" listing using the navigation link or breadcrumb.
Click the category tab labeled "Communication & Electronics".
In this category list, click the "Add to Learning Path" icon on the first article card whose title includes "VHF".
After adding the three articles, click the "My Learning Path" link in the header or sidebar to open the page that shows your custom three-step learning path.

tasks.name: Create a study list with three radio distress call terms from the glossary
tasks.description: Use the glossary to find and save the terms Mayday, Pan-Pan, and Sécurité (or Securite) into your personal Study List, then open the Study List view.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Glossary" option in the top navigation bar.
On the Glossary page, click into the glossary search box, type "mayday", and submit the search using Enter or the search icon.
In the search results, click the "Add to Study List" icon next to the term "Mayday".
Clear the search field, type "pan-pan" into the glossary search box, and run the search again.
Click the "Add to Study List" icon next to the term "Pan-Pan".
Clear the search field once more, type "securite" into the search box, and run the search.
Click the "Add to Study List" icon next to the term "Sécurité" or "Securite" (whichever appears).
Click the "My Study List" link or tab near the top of the page to open the view that lists your saved glossary terms.
